questionText: Solve $\operatorname{cosec}^2 x - 3\cot x - 5 = 0$ for $0 \le x < 360^\circ$. Give any non-exact answers to one decimal place.

steps.description: State the equation and note the domain
steps.explanation: Here $\operatorname{cosec}^2 x$ and $\cot x$ appear together. Converting $\operatorname{cosec}^2 x$ into $\cot x$ will make the whole equation a quadratic in $\cot x$.
The identity $1 + \cot^2 x \equiv \operatorname{cosec}^2 x$ lets us replace $\operatorname{cosec}^2 x$ so that only $\cot x$ remains.
After substituting, the equation involves only $\cot x$, which is exactly the single ratio we want.
Combining the constants ($1 - 5 = -4$) gives a tidy quadratic in $\cot x$.
Letting $u = \cot x$ reveals a standard quadratic to factorise.
Two numbers with product $-4$ and sum $-3$ are $-4$ and $+1$, giving this factorisation.
The cotangent, like the tangent, can take any real value, so both roots are attainable; nothing is rejected here.
Since $\cot x = \tfrac{1}{\tan x}$, we invert each value to work with $\tan x$, which is easier to solve on a calculator.
The base angle comes from the calculator. Tangent is positive in the first and third quadrants, so solutions are $180^\circ$ apart.
Adding the period $180^\circ$ to the base angle gives the third-quadrant solution. Both lie in the interval.
The reference angle is $45^\circ$; tangent is negative in the second and fourth quadrants, giving the exact solutions $135^\circ$ and $315^\circ$.
Combining the two cases gives four solutions across the interval.
At $x = 135^\circ$ the left-hand side evaluates to $0$, matching the equation and confirming the working.
These four angles are the full solution set in $0 \le x < 360^\circ$.
